subject: April Must-Reads: Context Layers, Claude Cowork, and More
body_html:
Don't miss our most popular articles from the past month! ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏  ͏ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­ ­  

The Variable

April Must-Reads: Context Layers, Claude Cowork, and More

Efficiency. Productivity. Speed. When everyone's relying on the same LLM- and agent-powered tools, both teams and individual practitioners are trying to set themselves apart by optimizing their level of execution.

 

That, at least, is the sense we're getting from April's most-read articles, which we're thrilled to share with you this week.

 

From a context layer that makes LLM systems truly click to the force-multiplying potential of autonomous agents, our authors have been busy sharing hands-on insights to help you excel at your work. Let's dive in.

RAG Isn’t Enough — I Built the Missing Context Layer That Makes LLM Systems Work

RAG systems become fragile as soon as they go live and face real-world memory and token constraints (among a slew of other breaking points). Emmimal P Alexander introduces us to a potential solution: "between raw retrieval and prompt construction, there’s a deliberate architectural step: deciding what the model actually sees, how much of it, and in what order."

Read More

How to Maximize Claude Cowork

If you'd like to reap the benefits of agentic automation without the full-blown programming capabilities of Claude Code, don't miss Eivind Kjosbakken's intro to Cowork, which comes with a more accessible UI. 

Read More

Using OpenClaw as a Force Multiplier: What One Person Can Ship with Autonomous Agents

Follow along Nick Lawson's "builder's journal" to get a concrete sense of what you can actually accomplish with agents.

Read More

Other April Highlights

Here are a few more of last month's most popular stories, covering topics like LLM best practices, learning Python, and agent skills, among others:

  • How to Learn Python for Data Science Fast in 2026 (Without Wasting Time), by Egor Howell
    What I wish I did at the beginning of my journey.
     
  • 6 Things I Learned Building LLMs From Scratch That No Tutorial Teaches You, by Aman Vasisht
    A statistical and architectural deep dive into the optimizations powering modern Transformers.

  • Beyond Prompting: Using Agent Skills in Data Science, by Yu Dong
    How I turned my eight-year weekly visualization habit into a reusable AI workflow.

  • A Visual Explanation of Linear Regression, by Mikhail Sarafanov
    How to build linear regression models, measure their quality, and improve their performance.

  • Advanced RAG Retrieval: Cross-Encoders & Reranking, by Ian Ho
     A deep-dive and practical guide to cross-encoders, advanced techniques, and why your retrieval pipeline deserves a second pass.

  • What Does the p-value Even Mean?, by Sara A. Metwalli
    What the essential statistical measure actually tells us.

Live Sessions from Our Sister Site, The New Stack

Vectors Aren't Enough. Go Beyond.

Vector embeddings changed search, but they represent only a single point in space. For complex, multimodal, or highly nuanced ranking, you need more dimensions. You need tensors. Join this live webinar for a concise, practical primer on why tensors are the next leap for production AI.

Register Today

Is AI Actually Slowing You Down? 

Companies are pouring money into AI tools, yet software delivery isn’t getting any faster. Why? Because AI-generated code is being throttled by manual pipelines, security hurdles, and outdated deployment processes. Learn why your delivery foundation matters more than your AI model.

Register Today

Authors in the Spotlight

We hope you take the time to read our recent author Q&A, and explore top-notch work from our newest contributors:

  • In our latest Author Spotlight, we chatted with Sabrine Bendimerad about the changing terrain of career paths today, and the enduring importance of human thinking in the age of agents.

  • Sayali Patil presented an accessible and thorough introduction to chaos engineering in the context of AI.

  • Omer Rosenbaum shared a hands-on guide to mastering Git UNDO, for all those moments when rewriting Git history is necessary.

Contribute to TDS

Did your New Year's resolutions include publishing on TDS and joining our Author Payment Program? It's not too late to send along your draft!
Submit Your Article

Want more insights, industry trends, and exclusive content? Follow us on social media for real-time updates and expert discussions!

Vectors Aren't Enough. Go Beyond.

Vector embeddings changed search, but they represent only a single point in space. For complex, multimodal, or highly nuanced ranking, you need more dimensions. You need tensors. Join this live webinar for a concise, practical primer on why tensors are the next leap for production AI.

Is AI Actually Slowing You Down?

Companies are pouring money into AI tools, yet software delivery isn’t getting any faster. Why? Because AI-generated code is being throttled by manual pipelines, security hurdles, and outdated deployment processes. Learn why your delivery foundation matters more than your AI model.
